steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: A percent increase of 584% means the mass grew by 584% of its original value. The new mass equals original plus increase: $k + 5.84k = 6.84k$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Equivalently, multiply by the growth factor: $k \times (1 + 584/100) = k \times 6.84 = 6.84k$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
• The matching choice: $6.84k$ — equals $k \times (1 + 5.84)$, correctly applying a 584% increase. A is correct.
  • $5.84k$ — omits the original mass; this represents only the increase, not the final mass. Incorrect.
  • $1.07k$ — corresponds to a 7% increase, not 584%. Incorrect.
  • $0.07k$ — represents 7% of the original mass, not the final mass after a 584% increase. Incorrect.
